methodology: Recency + impact signals + cross-source corroboration + geographic diversity

items.title: New strikes test the Iran ceasefire
items.summary: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is being tested after new attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. Mideast expert Paul Salem explains what's happened and whether the deal can still hold.

items.title: Reporter's Notebook: What its like to report from an Ebola outbreak
items.summary: Covering an Ebola outbreak means taking extra precautions. In this Reporter's Notebook, NPR's Emmet Livingstone takes us behind the reporting in eastern Congo.

items.title: Venezuela races to find survivors
items.summary: The search for survivors is entering a critical stage in Venezuela. NPR'S Eyder Peralta reports from La Guaira where rescue crews are still hoping to find people alive.

items.title: Uzbekistan makes its World Cup debut, a first for Central Asia
items.summary: The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."

items.title: Venezuelans in Colombia scramble to send aid as earthquakes death toll increases
items.summary: Aid efforts are intensifying after twin earthquakes killed over 1,400 in Venezuela, with international teams arriving but a slow government response hampering relief on the ground.

items.title: Inside a secretive Ukrainian team launching deep drone strikes at Russia
items.summary: Ukraine's long-range drones are striking deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries and depots. NPR recently spent time with one of the Ukrainian strike teams launching drones at Russian targets.

items.title: A historian analyzes whether Ukraine has a localized advantage in modern combat power
items.summary: NPR's Scott Simon talks with strategic studies professor Phillips O'Brien of St Andrew's University in Scotland about whether Ukraine has gained the upper hand in the fight against Russia.

items.title: U.S. and Iran each announce retaliatory strikes in Iran, Kuwait and Bahrain
items.summary: A recent ceasefire agreement between Washington and Tehran appears even more fragile following the latest hostilities in the region, which includes U.S. strikes on Iranian military targets.

items.title: There's a beef about beef at the World Cup, as Argentina fans pour into Texas
items.summary: It's about who produces the best, most succulent steaks, and how to prepare the meat. Argentina and Texas are two of the top cattle-raising areas of the world, where steak is deeply ingrained in diet and culture.

items.title: UN suspends Strait of Hormuz evacuation after ship 'hit by projectile'
items.summary: The UN has halted the evacuation of thousands of seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel came under a suspected attack.

items.title: U.S. pledges generous earthquake relief to Venezuela
items.summary: Following the earthquakes in Venezuela, the Trump administration announced what appears to be its strongest disaster response since it dismantled America's premier aid agency USAID last year.

items.title: Thirteen injured and pilot killed as plane hits Beijing's tallest building
items.summary: Thirteen people were injured and the pilot was killed when a light aircraft crashed into a Beijing skyscraper on Friday.

items.title: Christmas market terrorist who drove into crowds jailed for life
items.summary: A man who killed six people when he drove into crowds at a Christmas market in Germany has been sentenced to life in prison.

items.title: British TikToker could face death penalty after Dubai murder charge
items.summary: A British TikToker has been charged with premeditated murder in Dubai and could face the death penalty if convicted.
